annotations.label: Polytope

anchor.snippet: a polytope is a geometric object with flat sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general `Polytope` object; only the convex-hull operator underlying convex polytopes exists.

annotations.label: Polygon and polyhedron as polytopes

anchor.snippet: a two-dimensional polygon is a 2-polytope and a three-dimensional polyhedron is a 3-polytope

annotations.note: No dimension-indexed `n`-polytope notion exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polytope via simplicial decomposition

anchor.snippet: a polytope is the union of finitely many simplices

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Affine.Simplex` but no definition of a polytope as a finite union of simplices.

annotations.label: Polytope as bounded polyhedron (alternate terminology)

anchor.snippet: polytope means a bounded polyhedron

annotations.note: No `Polyhedron`/`Polytope` definitions exist to support this terminological convention.

annotations.label: Convex polyhedron vs convex polytope

anchor.snippet: a convex polyhedron is the intersection of a finite number of halfspaces

annotations.note: Intersection-of-finitely-many-halfspaces is formalized only for cones (`PointedCone.DualFG`), not general convex polyhedra.

annotations.label: Facets and ridges

anchor.snippet: An n -dimensional polytope is bounded by a number of

annotations.note: Mathlib has no polytope face lattice, so codimension-1 facets and codimension-2 ridges are undefined.

annotations.label: Vertex, edge, face, cell

anchor.snippet: A 0-dimensional face is called a vertex

annotations.note: Vertices correspond to `Set.extremePoints`/exposed points, but graded faces of higher dimension (edge, face, cell) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Convex polytope

anchor.snippet: A convex polytope is sometimes defined as the intersection of a set of half-spaces

annotations.note: The convex-hull-of-finite-set description appears informally (e.g. in `Convex.exists_subset_interior_convexHull_finset_of_isCompact`), but no bundled convex-polytope type exists.

annotations.label: Bounded polytope

anchor.snippet: A polytope is bounded if there is a ball of finite radius that contains it

annotations.note: The general notion of a bounded set (contained in a ball) is formalized, but not as a polytope property.

annotations.label: Pointed polytope

anchor.snippet: A polytope is said to be pointed if it contains at least one vertex

annotations.note: No notion of a pointed polytope exists (the unrelated `PointedCone` concerns cones, not vertex-possession).

annotations.label: Bounded nonempty polytope is pointed

anchor.snippet: Every bounded nonempty polytope is pointed

annotations.note: The Krein–Milman lemma shows a nonempty compact set has an extreme point, the closest analogue, but the polytope-specific statement is absent.

annotations.label: Non-pointed polytope

anchor.snippet: An example of a non-pointed polytope is the set

annotations.note: No polytope theory exists in which to state this example.

annotations.label: Finite polytope

anchor.snippet: A polytope is finite if it is defined in terms of a finite number of objects

annotations.note: No `Polytope` definition exists, so finiteness of a polytope is not formalized.

annotations.label: Integral polytope

anchor.snippet: It is an integral polytope if all of its vertices have integer coordinates

annotations.note: No lattice/integral-polytope notion exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflexive polytope

anchor.snippet: A certain class of convex polytopes are reflexive polytopes

annotations.note: Reflexive polytopes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflexive lattice-point criterion

anchor.snippet: It follows from this definition that

annotations.note: No reflexive-polytope theory, so this lattice-point criterion is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reflexive iff dual is integral

anchor.snippet: is reflexive if and only if its dual polytope

annotations.note: Neither reflexive polytopes nor dual polytopes are formalized.

annotations.label: Regular polytope flag-transitivity and self-dual regularity

anchor.snippet: acts transitively on its flags

annotations.note: Regular polytopes and their flag-transitive symmetry groups are not formalized as polytopes.

annotations.label: Three main classes of regular polytope

anchor.snippet: There are three main classes of regular polytope which occur in any number of dimensions

annotations.note: Coxeter matrices (`CoxeterMatrix.Aₙ`, `Bₙ`) name the symmetry types but the simplex/hypercube/cross-polytope families and their classification are not formalized.

annotations.label: No other regular polytopes in higher dimensions

anchor.snippet: But in higher dimensions there are no other regular polytopes

annotations.note: The classification theorem for regular polytopes is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nine regular polyhedra in three dimensions

anchor.snippet: bringing the total to nine regular polyhedra

annotations.note: The enumeration of the nine regular polyhedra (Platonic plus Kepler–Poinsot) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sixteen regular 4-polytopes

anchor.snippet: giving in all sixteen regular 4-polytopes

annotations.note: The enumeration of regular 4-polytopes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Star polytope

anchor.snippet: this class of polytopes include the star polytopes

annotations.note: Star polytopes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of polytope boundary

anchor.snippet: dimensions is contractible to a point

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Algebra.Homology.EulerCharacteristic` is homological and unconnected to a polytope's face-count V−E+F formula.

annotations.label: Gram–Euler theorem

anchor.snippet: The Gram–Euler theorem similarly generalizes the alternating sum of internal angles

annotations.note: The Gram–Euler theorem on alternating sums of internal angles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Apeirotopes (infinite polytopes)

anchor.snippet: and are sometimes called apeirotopes because they have infinitely many cells

annotations.note: Apeirotopes/infinite polytopes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Abstract polytope

anchor.snippet: An abstract polytope is a partially ordered set of elements or members

annotations.note: Mathlib has graded/ranked orders (`GradeOrder`, noted as the `Fin n`-graded setting of polytopes) but not the diamond/connectivity axioms defining an abstract polytope.

annotations.label: Realization of an abstract polytope

anchor.snippet: A geometric polytope is said to be a realization in some real space of the associated abstract polytope

annotations.note: Realizations of abstract polytopes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex polytope

anchor.snippet: Structures analogous to polytopes exist in complex Hilbert spaces

annotations.note: Complex polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual polytope

anchor.snippet: Every n -polytope has a dual structure, obtained by interchanging its vertices for facets

annotations.note: Polytope duality (interchanging vertices and facets) is not formalized; only order/cone duals exist.

annotations.label: Polytopes exist in dual pairs

anchor.snippet: Thus, polytopes exist in dual pairs

annotations.note: Without polytope duality, the dual-pair statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Self-dual polytope

anchor.snippet: the dual figure will be similar to the original and the polytope is self-dual

annotations.note: Self-dual polytopes are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Common self-dual polytopes

anchor.snippet: Some common self-dual polytopes include

annotations.note: No examples of self-dual polytopes are formalized.
